audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Thessalonians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for thanksgiving, work of trust, labor of love, endurance of hope, imitation, turning from idols, waiting for the Son.

verses.restored: Paul and Silvanus and Timothy, to the assembly of Thessalonians in Divine Father and Lord Jesus Messiah: grace to you and peace.
We give thanks to the Divine always concerning all of you, making remembrance in our prayers,
unceasingly remembering your work of trust, labor of love, and endurance of hope in our Lord Jesus Messiah before our Divine and Father,
knowing, siblings beloved by the Divine, your choosing,
because our good news did not come to you in word only, but also in power and in holy Spirit and in much full assurance, just as you know what sort we became among you for your sake.
And you became imitators of us and of the Lord, receiving the word in much pressure with joy of holy Spirit,
so that you became a pattern to all those trusting in Macedonia and in Achaia.
For from you the word of the Lord has sounded out, not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but in every place your trust toward the Divine has gone out, so that we have no need to say anything.
For they themselves report concerning us what sort of entrance we had toward you, and how you turned to the Divine from idols, to serve a living and true Divine,
and to wait for his Son from the heavens, whom he raised from the dead, Jesus, the One rescuing us from the coming wrath.

translation_notes: The triad in 1:3 is preserved: work of trust, labor of love, endurance of hope.
Pressure and joy in holy Spirit should both remain visible; this is not triumphalism but resilient reception.
